comment_text.sn15.13:1.2: The same thirty monks from Pāvā visited the Buddha on another occasion when he was at Sāvatthī, occasioning the allowance for the robe-making ceremony after the rains residence (Kd 7:1.1.1). | Pāvā, a town of the Mallas, was where Mahāvīra died, plunging the Jains into chaos. (The Jains, however, say this was another Pāvā east of Nāḷandā.) Perhaps because of this, Pāvā became associated with especially ascetic monks such as those in this discourse: Mahākassapa heard the news of the Buddha’s passing at Pāvā; and sixty monks from Pāvā allied with monks of “Avanti and the south” arguing for strict Vinaya in the Second Council (Kd 22:1.7.11.1).
comment_text.sn15.13:1.4: See SN 16.5:2.1 for explanations of these strict observances.

root_text.sn15.13:1.2: Atha kho tiṁsamattā pāveyyakā bhikkhū sabbe āraññikā sabbe piṇḍapātikā sabbe paṁsukūlikā sabbe tecīvarikā sabbe sasaṁyojanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.sn15.13:1.4: “ime kho tiṁsamattā pāveyyakā bhikkhū sabbe āraññikā sabbe piṇḍapātikā sabbe paṁsukūlikā sabbe tecīvarikā sabbe sasaṁyojanā.
root_text.sn15.13:1.5: Yannūnāhaṁ imesaṁ tathā dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ yathā nesaṁ imasmiṁyeva āsane anupādāya āsavehi cittāni vimucceyyun”ti.
root_text.sn15.13:2.2: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.13:2.3: Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, bhikkhave, katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yaṁ vā vo iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, yaṁ vā catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakan”ti?
root_text.sn15.13:2.4: “Yathā kho mayaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāma, etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ, yaṁ no iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakan”ti.
root_text.sn15.13:3.1: “Sādhu sādhu, bhikkhave, sādhu kho me tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānātha.
root_text.sn15.13:3.2: Etadeva, bhikkhave, bahutaraṁ, yaṁ vo iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.
root_text.sn15.13:3.3: Dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, gunnaṁ sataṁ gobhūtānaṁ sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.
root_text.sn15.13:3.4: Dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, mahiṁsānaṁ sataṁ mahiṁsabhūtānaṁ sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn15.13:3.5: dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, urabbhānaṁ sataṁ urabbhabhūtānaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn15.13:3.10: dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, corā gāmaghātāti gahetvā sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ.
root_text.sn15.13:3.11: Dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, corā pāripanthikāti gahetvā sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ.
root_text.sn15.13:3.12: Dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, corā pāradārikāti gahetvā sīsacchinnānaṁ lohitaṁ passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.
root_text.sn15.13:4.2: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.
root_text.sn15.13:4.3: Imasmiñca pana veyyākaraṇasmiṁ bhaññamāne tiṁsamattānaṁ pāveyyakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ anupādāya āsavehi cittāni vimucciṁsūti.

translation_text.sn15.13:1.1: Near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove.
translation_text.sn15.13:1.2: Then thirty mendicants from Pāvā went to the Buddha. All of them lived in the wilderness, ate only almsfood, wore rag robes, and owned just three robes; yet they all still had fetters. They bowed to the Buddha and sat down to one side.
translation_text.sn15.13:1.3: Then it occurred to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn15.13:1.4: “These thirty mendicants from Pāvā live in the wilderness, eat only almsfood, wear rag robes, and own just three robes; yet they all still have fetters.
translation_text.sn15.13:1.5: Why don’t I teach them the Dhamma in such a way that their minds are freed from defilements by not grasping while sitting in this very seat?”
translation_text.sn15.13:1.6: Then the Buddha said to the mendicants,
translation_text.sn15.13:2.1: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.13:2.2: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.13:2.3: What do you think? Which is more: the flow of blood you’ve shed when your head was chopped off while roaming and transmigrating for such a very long time, or the water in the four oceans?”
translation_text.sn15.13:2.4: “As we understand the Buddha’s teaching, the flow of blood we’ve shed when our head was chopped off while roaming and transmigrating is more than the water in the four oceans.”
translation_text.sn15.13:3.1: “Good, good, mendicants! It’s good that you understand my teaching like this.
translation_text.sn15.13:3.2: The flow of blood you’ve shed when your head was chopped off while roaming and transmigrating is indeed more than the water in the four oceans.
translation_text.sn15.13:3.3: For a long time you’ve been cows, and the flow of blood you’ve shed when your head was chopped off as a cow is more than the water in the four oceans.
translation_text.sn15.13:3.4: For a long time you’ve been buffalo …
translation_text.sn15.13:3.10: For a long time you’ve been bandits, arrested for raiding villages,
translation_text.sn15.13:3.12: or adultery. And the flow of blood you’ve shed when your head was chopped off as a bandit is more than the water in the four oceans.
translation_text.sn15.13:3.14: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.13:3.15: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
translation_text.sn15.13:4.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn15.13:4.2: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn15.13:4.3: And while this discourse was being spoken, the minds of the thirty mendicants from Pāvā were freed from defilements by not grasping.

variant_text.sn15.13:3.4: mahiṁsānaṁ → mahisānaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
